name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلَىٰ تِجَـٰرَةٍ تُنجِيكُم مِّنْ عَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (١٠)
```

```english
O you who have faith! Shall I show you a deal that will deliver you from a painful punishment? (10)
```

```arabic
تُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَتُجَـٰهِدُونَ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ بِأَمْوَٰلِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (١١)
```

```english
Have faith in Allah and His Apostle, and wage jihad in the way of Allah with your persons and possessions. That is better for you, should you know. (11)
```

```arabic
يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَيُدْخِلْكُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ وَمَسَـٰكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِى جَنَّـٰتِ عَدْنٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (١٢)
```

```english
He will forgive your sins and admit you into gardens with streams running in them, and into good dwellings in the Gardens of Eden; that is the great success. (12)
```

```arabic
وَأُخْرَىٰ تُحِبُّونَهَا ۖ نَصْرٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَفَتْحٌ قَرِيبٌ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (١٣)
```

```english
And other [blessings] you cherish: help from Allah and a victory near at hand, and give good news to the faithful. (13)
```

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُونُوٓا۟ أَنصَارَ ٱللَّهِ كَمَا قَالَ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ لِلْحَوَارِيِّـۧنَ مَنْ أَنصَارِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنصَارُ ٱللَّهِ ۖ فَـَٔامَنَت طَّآئِفَةٌ مِّنۢ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَكَفَرَت طَّآئِفَةٌ ۖ فَأَيَّدْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ عَلَىٰ عَدُوِّهِمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا۟ ظَـٰهِرِينَ (١٤)
```

```english
O you who have faith! Be Allah’s helpers, just as Jesus son of Mary said to his disciples, ‘Who will be my helpers for Allah’s sake?’ The Disciples said, ‘We will be Allah’s helpers!’ So a group of the Children of Israel believed, and a group disbelieved. Then We strengthened the faithful against their enemies, and they came to prevail [over them]. (14)
```

## (Commentary)

This is a call to the believers (المؤمنين) toward faith (الإيمان) in Allah and His Messenger, and toward struggle (الجهاد) in the way of Allah. It contains a beautiful promise of forgiveness and Paradise in the Hereafter, and of victory and conquest in this world. It is also a call for them to remain firm in their support for Allah, accompanied by a gracious promise of divine assistance.

These two meanings constitute the ultimate purpose of the Surah, while the preceding verses serve as a preamble and introduction to them.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى تِجارَةٍ تُنْجِيكُمْ مِنْ عَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ** **"O you who have faith! Shall I show you a trade that will deliver you from a painful punishment?"** (61:10). The interrogative form here is used for offering (العرض), and it carries the sense of a command.

**Trade** (التجارة) — according to what al-Raghib mentioned — is the disposal of capital (رأس المال) in pursuit of profit (الربح). In the Arabic language, there is no word where the letter *ta* (تاء) is followed by *jim* (جيم) except for this term.

Thus, faith (الإيمان) and struggle (الجهاد) in this verse are treated as a trade whose capital is the soul (النفس) and whose profit is deliverance from a painful punishment. The verse is similar in meaning to His saying: **إِنَّ اللهَ اشْتَرى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوالَهُمْ بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ يُقاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَيَقْتُلُونَ وَيُقْتَلُونَ** — until He said — **فَاسْتَبْشِرُوا بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بايَعْتُمْ بِهِ** **"Indeed Allah has bought from the faithful their souls and their possessions for Paradise being theirs: they fight in the way of Allah, so they slay and are slain... So rejoice in the bargain you have made with Him"** (9:111).

The Exalted has magnified the status of this trade by saying: **عَلى تِجارَةٍ** "in a trade," meaning a trade of great worth and immense significance. He made the resulting profit the deliverance from a painful punishment whose magnitude cannot be estimated.

The manifestation of this promised deliverance is forgiveness and Paradise; therefore, He subsequently substituted "deliverance from punishment" with His saying: **يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَيُدْخِلْكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ** "He will forgive you your sins and admit you into gardens..." As for the promised victory and conquest, they are distinct from the promised deliverance; hence, He separated them from forgiveness and Paradise, saying: **وَأُخْرى تُحِبُّونَها نَصْرٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَفَتْحٌ قَرِيبٌ** **"And other [blessings] you love: help from Allah and a victory near at hand"** (61:13). So do not be heedless of this distinction.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُجاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ بِأَمْوالِكُمْ وَأَنْفُسِكُمْ** **"That you have faith in Allah and His Apostle and wage jihad in the way of Allah with your possessions and your persons"** (61:11). This is an explanatory commencement (استئناف بياني) that clarifies the trade offered to them, as if it were asked: "What is this trade?" and the reply was: "That you have faith in Allah and His Messenger and wage struggle..." Faith in the Messenger was mentioned alongside faith in Allah to indicate the obligation of obeying him in what he commands; otherwise, faith is not considered faith in Allah except when accompanied by faith in the messengership of the Messenger. The Exalted said: **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِاللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ وَيُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يُفَرِّقُوا بَيْنَ اللهِ وَرُسُلِهِ** — until He said — **أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْكافِرُونَ حَقًّا** **"Those who deny Allah and His apostles and seek to separate Allah from His apostles... it is they who are truly faithless"** (4:150-151).

His saying: **ذلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ** **"That is better for you, should you know"** (61:11) means that what has been mentioned of faith and struggle is better for you if you are among the people of knowledge (أهل العلم); as for the ignorant, their deeds are of no account.

It has also been said: The meaning is that you will know the goodness of this if you are among the people of knowledge and deep understanding (الفقه).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ وَيُدْخِلْكُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ** **"He will forgive you your sins and admit you into gardens with streams running in them"** (61:12). This is the apodosis (جواب) for the implied condition (الشرط المقدر) understood from the previous verse; that is: "If you believe in Allah and His Messenger and struggle in His way, He will forgive you..."

The sins (الذنوب) to which forgiveness pertains are mentioned unconditionally (أطلقت), implying that all sins are forgiven. Reason supports this, as this forgiveness is a prerequisite for entering the Garden of Eternity (جنة الخلد), and there is no sense in entering it while some sins remain as they were. Perhaps to point toward this subtle point, He followed it with His saying: **وَمَساكِنَ طَيِّبَةً فِي جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ** **"and goodly dwellings in the Gardens of Eden"** (61:12), meaning gardens of permanence and stability. Their being a place of permanence and a site of stability suggests that the forgiveness pertains to all sins.

Additionally, there is the correspondence between the soul that is sacrificed—which is a meager, fleeting commodity—and the Gardens of Eden, which are eternal. Through this, the soul of the believer finds comfort and his will is strengthened to sacrifice the soul and choose the eternal over the ephemeral.

Then He further emphasized this by saying: **ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ** **"That is the great triumph"** (61:12).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَأُخْرى تُحِبُّونَها نَصْرٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَفَتْحٌ قَرِيبٌ** **"And other [blessings] you love: help from Allah and a victory near at hand"** (61:13). This is coordinated (عطف) with His saying: **يَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ** "He will forgive you." The word **أُخْرى** "other" is an adjective standing in place of the noun it describes, and it is the predicate (خبر) of a deleted subject (مبتدء). His saying: **نَصْرٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَفَتْحٌ قَرِيبٌ** "help from Allah and a victory near at hand" is an explanation (بيان) for "other." The implied meaning is: "And for you is another blessing or trait that you love, which is help from Allah and a near, immediate victory."

His saying: **وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ** **"and give good news to the faithful"** (61:13) is coordinated with the command understood from the preceding speech, as if it were said: "Say: O you who have faith, shall I show you..." and "give good news to the faithful."

This glad tiding (البشرى) corresponds to what is in His saying: **إِنَّ اللهَ اشْتَرى مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَأَمْوالَهُمْ بِأَنَّ لَهُمُ الْجَنَّةَ** — until He said — **فَاسْتَبْشِرُوا بِبَيْعِكُمُ الَّذِي بايَعْتُمْ بِهِ** **"Indeed Allah has bought from the faithful their souls and their possessions for Paradise being theirs... So rejoice in the bargain you have made with Him"** (9:111). From this, it becomes clear that what he was commanded to give glad tidings of is the totality of the reward Allah grants them in the Hereafter and this world, not specifically victory and conquest.

All of this is what the context (السياق) provides regarding the meaning of the verse and the grammatical analysis of its parts. Other matters have been mentioned regarding it which the context does not support as strongly, so we have refrained from mentioning them. It is also possible that His saying: **وَبَشِّرِ** "and give good news" is a new sentence (استئناف).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُونُوا أَنْصارَ اللهِ** **"O you who have faith! Be Allah’s helpers"** (61:14), meaning: take on this mark (السمة), persevere in it, and remain firm upon it. The verse is in the sense of an intensification (الترقي) relative to His previous saying: **هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى تِجارَةٍ تُنْجِيكُمْ مِنْ عَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ** "Shall I show you a trade that will deliver you from a painful punishment?" The ultimate meaning is: Trade with your souls and your wealth, thus helping Allah through faith and struggle in His way, and persevere and remain firm in His support.

What is meant by their helping Allah is that they help His Prophet in traversing the path he takes toward Allah with insight (بصيرة), as He said: **قُلْ هذِهِ سَبِيلِي أَدْعُوا إِلَى اللهِ عَلى بَصِيرَةٍ أَنَا وَمَنِ اتَّبَعَنِي** **"Say, ‘This is my way: I summon to Allah with insight—I and he who follows me’"** (12:108).

The evidence for this meaning is His drawing a parallel between His saying: **كُونُوا أَنْصارَ اللهِ** "Be Allah's helpers" and His subsequent saying: **كَما قالَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ لِلْحَوارِيِّينَ مَنْ أَنْصارِي إِلَى اللهِ قالَ الْحَوارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنْصارُ اللهِ** **"as Jesus son of Mary said to the Disciples, ‘Who will be my helpers toward Allah?’ The Disciples said, ‘We will be Allah’s helpers’"** (61:14). The Disciples (الحواريون) being Allah's helpers means they were helpers to Jesus son of Mary (peace be upon him) in his traversing the path of Allah and his turning toward Allah, which is monotheism (التوحيد) — *the absolute oneness of Allah in His essence, attributes, and acts* — and the sincerity of worship (العبادة) to Allah, the Glorified. Thus, the correspondence of their saying: "We are Allah's helpers" to his saying: "Who are my helpers toward Allah?" and its conformity with it necessitates the unity of the meaning of the two phrases according to the intended purpose. Therefore, those addressed by His saying: "O you who have faith! Be Allah's helpers" being helpers to Allah means they are helpers to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) in spreading the call and exalting the word of truth through struggle. This constitutes faith in the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) and obedience to him in what he commands and forbids, stemming from firm conviction and sincere action—as is the import of the context of the Surah's verses.

His saying: **فَآمَنَتْ طائِفَةٌ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ وَكَفَرَتْ طائِفَةٌ فَأَيَّدْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا عَلى عَدُوِّهِمْ فَأَصْبَحُوا ظاهِرِينَ** **"So a faction of the Children of Israel believed and a faction disbelieved. Then We strengthened those who believed against their enemies, and they became the ones who prevailed"** (61:14). This is an allusion to what occurred and how the matter of Jesus seeking help and the Disciples' response concluded, where the people split into a believing faction and a disbelieving one. Then Allah supported the believers against their enemy—the disbelievers—so they became prevalent after having been in hiding and oppressed.

In this, there is an intimation that within the community of the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), there will occur what occurred in the community of Jesus (peace be upon him): a faction of them will believe and a faction will disbelieve. If the believers respond to his call for help—and He, the Exalted, has stood in his place in seeking help to magnify his affair and honor him—Allah will support them against their enemy, and they will become prevalent just as the helpers of Jesus and those who believed in him prevailed.

The Exalted alluded to this story at the end of the accounts of Jesus (peace be upon him) in Surah Al-Imran, where He said: **فَلَمَّا أَحَسَّ عِيسى مِنْهُمُ الْكُفْرَ قالَ مَنْ أَنْصارِي إِلَى اللهِ قالَ الْحَوارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنْصارُ اللهِ** **"When Jesus sensed faithlessness on their part, he said, ‘Who will be my helpers toward Allah?’ The Disciples said, ‘We will be Allah’s helpers’"** (3:52), through to the end of six verses. By reflecting upon them, the meaning of the verse under discussion becomes clear.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In the *Tafsir* of al-Qummi, in the narration of Abu al-Jarud from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of the Exalted: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ـ هَلْ أَدُلُّكُمْ عَلى تِجارَةٍ تُنْجِيكُمْ مِنْ عَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ** "O you who have faith! Shall I show you a trade that will deliver you from a painful punishment?" He said: "They said: 'If we knew what it was, we would surely sacrifice for it our wealth, our souls, and our children.' So Allah said: **تُؤْمِنُونَ بِاللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ـ وَتُجاهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ بِأَمْوالِكُمْ** 'That you have faith in Allah and His Apostle and wage jihad in the way of Allah with your possessions...' until His saying: **ذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْعَظِيمُ** 'That is the great triumph.'"

**I say:** This meaning is also narrated through Sunni channels.

In the same source, regarding the saying of the Exalted: **وَأُخْرى تُحِبُّونَها نَصْرٌ مِنَ اللهِ وَفَتْحٌ قَرِيبٌ** "And other [blessings] you love: help from Allah and a victory near at hand," he said: "Meaning in this world through the victory of the *Qa'im* (peace be upon him)." He also said: "The conquest of Mecca."

In *al-Ihtijaj*, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition: "And He did not leave His earth without a scholar (عالم) who knows what the creation needs, and a learner (متعلم) on the path of salvation—those are the fewest in number. Allah has clarified that regarding the nations of the prophets and made them an example for those who came after, such as His saying regarding the Disciples of Jesus—when he said to the rest of the Children of Israel: **مَنْ أَنْصارِي إِلَى اللهِ ـ قالَ الْحَوارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنْصارُ اللهِ آمَنَّا بِاللهِ ـ وَاشْهَدْ بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ** 'Who will be my helpers toward Allah?' The Disciples said, 'We will be Allah’s helpers. We have faith in Allah, and bear witness that we are Muslims'—meaning they are submissive (مسلمون) to the people of merit for their merit, and they do not act arrogantly toward the command of their Lord. None responded to him from among them except the Disciples."

**I say:** Although the narration was reported regarding the interpretation of the verse in Al-Imran, it is useful for our current discussion.

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Ibn Ishaq and Ibn Sa'd narrated from Abdullah ibn Abi Bakr ibn Muhammad ibn Amr ibn Hazm, who said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) said to the group who met him at al-Aqabah: 'Bring out to me twelve men from among you who shall be guarantors (كفلاء) over their people, just as the Disciples were guarantors for Jesus son of Mary.'"
